title: Board level discussion

posts.author: Lucas (Lucas de Melo)
posts.cookedHtml:
Dear Monday.com Team,

We use Slack + Monday.com for managing our Team remotely. We use Slack for discussing general topics about the work that doesn’t relate directly to a specific task. This leads to:

  • Spread Information
  • Desorganitaion

With Monday.com having its own chat room for every board would allow us to centralize everything in one tool and organize better the discussions.

  • Centralize Information
  • Everything at one unique tool (Monday.com)
  • Organization

Few organizational tools have it already and would like to see in our favorite tool monday.com.
Please consider that, it’s not a complex thing to ask for.

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
7 posts were merged into an existing topic: Chat & Messaging

10 votes have been moved. 7 votes could not be moved because their users already voted in the other topic.

posts.author: RBurgos (Rubén Burgos)
posts.cookedHtml:
When my team is on the go, they use either WhatsApp or iMessage for one-on-one & group convos.

I need to stop them.

Can we fast-track internal one-on-one, team & group chat for the app? It would also be useful to have a “create pulse” button because inevitably tasks will be extracted from these conversations.

Thank you!

posts.author: Patrick (patrick@parvenunext.com)
posts.cookedHtml:
We use monday for project management and CRM and Slack for internal messaging. They work really well together!

I suppose you could say monday could add messaging, but what’s the advantage of trying to take on Slack when they can already seamlessly integrate and work together?

You can create a pulse from monday with their Slack integration already. If you get Zapier you can do things like star a message in Slack to create a pulse on a particular monday board depending on the channel you posted in.

posts.author: RBurgos (Rubén Burgos)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’m of the belief that a Monday.com login should be as powerful as an Apple ID—one database of purchases & communications (and more) across any Apple Mac or iOS device.

Perhaps it’s the differing nature of our companies or company cultures or number of hats we need to wear daily, but the moment need to ask myself, “Did I discuss that with them in a Slack update or a Monday pulse?” I’ve already lost the efficiency battle.

Zapier is great. But I hit the free limit 12-15 days into the month. I need to control my budget by reducing the number of paid services we use. And the less segmented databases we need to juggle, the better.

So, if Monday.com doesn’t want to add an even basic level of internal messaging, they should say so, or this will continue being a suggestion you’ll need to proactively counter in the Monday Community boards.

Given whom my team comprises, Monday should be a one-stop shop (at least, I really want it to be). Slack doesn’t work for everyone. I’m glad it works for you! (For real! )

posts.author: Patrick (patrick@parvenunext.com)
posts.cookedHtml:
“Perhaps it’s the differing nature of our companies or company cultures or number of hats we need to wear daily, but the moment need to ask myself, “Did I discuss that with them in a Slack update or a Monday pulse?” I’ve already lost the efficiency battle.”

Monday has a native integration to update Slack channels for anything that happens in monday.

I’d also challenge that the time Zapier saves isn’t far more valuable than what it costs to move up from the free plan.

posts.author: TheOtherPM (Paul McGregor)
posts.cookedHtml:
I would also LOVE some internal chat functions inside Monday.com but for a different reason.

For me, it’s about the tech-literacy of our team.

My team have low-medium tech skills. Every time we introduce another app, there’s a huge lag between introduction and productivity. We have to upskill them in how to use the apps, constantly.

The fewer apps our team has to learn and navigate, the better.

posts.author: RBurgos (Rubén Burgos)
posts.cookedHtml: Such a great point! We also deal with this!

posts.author: mrdjallen (DJ Allen)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’d love to see an internal chat feature as well.

In similarity to previous points, our team already uses several applications in their daily routine, and every app that we can cut out keeps us all focused on a singular platform for notifications and weeds out a layer of complexity; less integration, less learning curve, and less designated spaces for simple things that could share a space.

posts.author: Thao.SFE (Thao T. )
posts.cookedHtml:
+1 on the internal chat feature. We currently use Facebook Messenger, and not all my employees have/want to use Facebook.

Would be nice to keep everything under Monday.com

posts.author: marco (Marco Albrecht)
posts.cookedHtml: I would love, if each monday folder stands for a channel in Slack or MS Teams. The general channel within the folder, and subchannels with the boards.

posts.author: colleenm (Colleen)
posts.cookedHtml:
This is a feature we’ve been dying for Monday to include.

We’re currently using email as our form of communication, and things get lost in the mix.

posts.author: JordanD (Jordan Di Conti)
posts.cookedHtml: Internal chat would be a great addition!

posts.author: alejencia (Alex Becerra)
posts.cookedHtml:
RBurgos,

I agree with you. Ever since we adopted Monday.com we’ve reduced our outlook emails by 80%. Communicating features such as chat inside monday.com would almost eliminate emails for us, it’ll be a one platform for all in our department.

I would also add that if such feature is developed, perhaps there can be a feature to attach certain chats to pulses.

posts.author: TheOtherPM (Paul McGregor)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey @Julia-monday.com I’m interested you know if your team has made a call about whether to include a chat feature in the roadmap? If not, I’ll need to work on a different solution with our team. If yes, ill just wait

posts.author: hlopezvc (Hernan Lopez)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 on adding internal chat on monday!!!

posts.author: Gauri (Kristelle (Gauri) Sim)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 (Can I do +100?) on adding a chat feature. That would make so much of a difference in terms of catching work-related info on our instant messages. Right now we use Telegram for everything in that area: simple, easy to use and learn, and lots of features. But lots of data gets lost in the mix. It would be wonderful not to have to learn something as formal as Slack just to integrate project info into Monday.com.

posts.author: Lucas (Lucas de Melo)
posts.cookedHtml:
Dear Monday.com Team,

We use Slack + Monday.com for managing our Team remotely. We use Slack for discussing general topics about the work that doesn’t relate directly to a specific task. This leads to:

  • Spread Information
  • Desorganitaion

With Monday.com having its own chat room for every board would allow us to centralize everything in one tool and organize better the discussions.

  • Centralize Information
  • Everything at one unique tool (Monday.com)
  • Organization

Few organizational tools have it already and would like to see in our favorite tool monday.com.
Please consider that, it’s not a complex thing to ask for.

posts.author: lindsay-monday.com (Lindsay Mayrose)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Lucas! Thanks so much for this suggestion. It is definitely something I will share with the product team.

That said, I’d like to highlight how many teams currently achieve this on monday.com. In order to have general conversations within a board that don’t necessarily apply to a specific task, we suggest creating a “General” group at the top of your board with an item called something like “Talks”, “Discussions”, “FAQs”, etc.

You can check out more about how this works in the following article under “High Level Communication”: https://support.monday.com/hc/en-us/articles/360010623059-Improving-Team-Communicating-with-monday-com

Hope this helps, and thanks again for this feedback!

posts.author: Jaewoo (Jaewoo Kim)
posts.cookedHtml:
Monday.com used to have this in an earlier version. But I was told they removed it. It was like having conversations at the board level instead of just at the pulse level like it is today.

I too would like this feature reintroduced & enhanced.

posts.author: Lucas (Lucas de Melo)
posts.cookedHtml:
Dear @lindsay-monday.com,

That suggestion could somehow work if the updates area would be more like a chat. The conversations box is huge, not in a way optimized for chatting. I am glad you consider this feature because I believe having its own chat platform inside Monday would require less integration and give a more complete solution for team managing.

best,

Lucas

posts.author: nealb (Neal Bailey)
posts.cookedHtml:
What I think would be useful is to not super complicate the app with adding a one on one chat, channels etc. After all slack and teams are battling that out.

I think however there is a few things that would be great.

  1. Ability to have internal chats like you can do on intercom, gist etc. This is where you make an update on a pulse and you switch to the internal or note or private tab (whatever you want to call it) so that guests (clients) do not see the update but it’s kept in context. For example Hey John can you review this file before we send to the customer.

  2. Allow chat at a board level (essentially a channel). This would be very helpful when making an update about the board in general.

  3. Updates on a dashboard, to allow discussions about the stats on the dashboard as now there is no way to do that and you must switch to slack or teams for this.

posts.author: kswiss50 (Kendall Shortt)
posts.cookedHtml: I agree. I am fairly new to the platform but I use Airtable everyday. I keep wanting to send a large group message on updates I am doing within the platform but I have to use email, which sucks.

posts.author: TheOtherPM (Paul McGregor)
posts.cookedHtml: Looks like they may be working on something. See the first feature mentioned 2/3 of the way down this article: https://monday.com/blog/remote-work/remote-hackathon-roadmap/

posts.author: kswiss50 (Kendall Shortt)
posts.cookedHtml: That is awesome! I gotta say I am impressed with the customer service and quick innovation of Monday.com. Glad to see they did a Hackathon just to push cool stuff through for us to use as we work from home.

posts.author: TuesdaysTools (Tomorrow's Tools)
posts.cookedHtml:
@RBurgos We have created Chat, a tool that automatically pulls in your Monday team and allows you to chat in public, private, and direct channels. You can also react with emoji and share images and files.

Check out it, we’d love some feedback and it’s still in beta Chat for Monday Teams

There’s an “Add to Monday” button you can use on this page

posts.author: TheOtherPM (Paul McGregor)
posts.cookedHtml: Looks promising. But what’s with the “minimum charge is for 5 users”? It’s a real frustration for a small business to pay for users that don’t exist.

posts.author: TheOtherPM (Paul McGregor)
posts.cookedHtml: Also @TuesdaysTools I have installed the Chat app but I don’t see how I access it. Can you help?

posts.author: anderslyman (Anders Lyman)
posts.cookedHtml:
@TheOtherPM That’s a fair question. Everyone that buys gets a dedicated chat server, and it’s a very real expense for us to deploy to and maintain. 5 users represents the bare-minimum price at which we can afford to operate a chat server.

As to installation, we’re working on an installation guide that we’ll put up on our website, but for now you’d add it just like you’d add any other dashboard widget:

  • Create a new, blank dashboard
  • No boards are needed, so just click “Done”
  • Add a widget (select “More”):
    image629×813 58.9 KB
  • Select “Account Apps”:
    image378×843 24.6 KB
  • Click “Add Widget”:
  • Click “Done”

That’s it - it’ll load Chat and log you in with your Monday account.

This is a publicly shared chat instance, so everyone that tries out the app will be here. The only restriction is that messages are deleted daily.

posts.author: anderslyman (Anders Lyman)
posts.cookedHtml:
There’s also an option to install it as a “Board View” - automatic fullscreen is the major advantage here, and it looks/works much better on mobile.

For that you’d pick any existing board, or create a new one, then navigate to the views dropdown, select “+ Add View” then “</> Apps”:

image840×1000 46.3 KB

Scroll to the bottom and you’ll see your account apps, find “Chat” and click “Add View”:

That’s pretty much it, it should load and log you in from there.

posts.author: TuesdaysTools (Tomorrow's Tools)
posts.cookedHtml:
@TheOtherPM - As Anders stated, we understand if there’s some frustration there. Many other chat services out there have minimum license seats required. Even monday has a minimum seat requirement of 3 users. Even at our min required seats, chat will cost you less than most any chat solution but the best part is we’re directly inside monday!

Happy to discuss further and give you a significant discount on your first year of usage since Chat is brand new. - contact@tomorrows-tools.com

posts.author: TheOtherPM (Paul McGregor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thanks @anderslyman and @TuesdaysTools for your explanations. I respect that re the data center costs.

We currently use Google Chat, which costs us nothing extra as part of G Suite. So that’s the comparison point for us.

Thanks again and I look forward to checking it out later

posts.author: anderslyman (Anders Lyman)
posts.cookedHtml:
@TheOtherPM that makes sense!

If Monday had a native chat system, then I bet it would come at no additional fee as well - so I do see what you mean.

  • The send button is in the righthand bottom corner, but it is often covered by the Monday black coloured icon looking like a hat, unless you click it away.
    This app is otherwise a welcome addition because I am trying to keep all conversations about a particular project in the designated project Monday space, but we do need to have private conversations that we think the client doesn’t need to know.

It can get overwhelming trying to sort out all of the updates, jumping around board to board, it would be nice to go to a chat and get ahold of someone instantly.

posts.author: flobau (Florian Bauer)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey, better a late reply than none.

It seems that chrome disables writing on localstorage for apps in the “avoid thrid party cookie” settings: Uncaught SecurityError: Failed to read the 'localStorage' property from 'Window': Access is denied for this document.

→ this app uses the key-value store of monday and uses localstorage to keep message drafts, so for now it only works when we can write to localstorage.
→ the other two points i will take a look at.

Thanks for the feedback

I’ve just found it and we haven’t tried it yet, but it looks promising - can have multiple threads going, etc.

posts.author: Wesley (Wesley Gant)
posts.cookedHtml:
The “Discussions” view is great, but it needs to also mirror any updates on specific pulses, or it becomes yet another place to look for where a conversation happened. If ALL discussion on a project shows in the Discussion view, there’s just one place to look.

In another topic thread I had suggested removing Inbox and turning notifications into a robust messaging and notifications center. That would be a great place to be able to see all of the messages coming in across monday, and click straight to the conversation.

If monday can do this well, and also allow DM threads, we could potentially ditch Slack. Human-to-Human communication is one of the most important areas for vastly improving this platform.

posts.author: TaylorTedesco (Taylor Tedesco)
posts.cookedHtml:
We tried out Discussions but it has too many issues that resulted in it not being helpful to us.

You can’t tag team names, so if I want to tag everyone I have to do so individually. Also the drop down menu to choose the people to tag cuts off on the side so you can’t fully read it.

If you get tagged, you get an email and notification about it but no context as to where to look so unless everyone remembers to specifically check there, people miss the info. No way to see if they saw it either.

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Lucas
Thanks for submitting this request and thanks to everyone who voted. While we currently are not planning on creating chat rooms for each individual board, we are working on developing a centralized place for communications on each board which will have similar capabilities.

P.S. Read more about our new feature request process and keep sharing your ideas with us!

posts.author: Danielle-monday.com (Danielle Glickman)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everybody!

We are currently working on a new feature that we think can help with this request- Board discussion! This feature allows to have a board level discussion and update area.

We are currently looking for beta users to test the feature before it comes out! If you are interested please fill out the following form: Beta accounts for Board Discussions

Thank you and hope to see you in our beta group!

posts.author: carlylaine (Carly )
posts.cookedHtml: What will it take to get this feature on the roadmap? Sometimes there are things to discuss that involve a much broader conversation than an item. Would be AMAZING to be able to have discussions/chats at a board level. Adding my voice to the mix in hopes of getting this some more traction.

posts.author: Tisha (TISHA CLARK)
posts.cookedHtml: This doesn’t seem it should be marked a solution yet. A lot of folks still interested in this happening.

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi community,

There are a few different comments and ideas around this request and the need for more discussion capabilities around boards.

  • Board level discussions are expected to be released in the coming months. With board level discussions you will be able to give general updates/ ideas and discussions around a specific boards.

-Chat rooms/channels are something we are considering for later this year but do not have plans for. We will make this as released but if you are still interested in furthering the request beyond board discussions. You can submit a new request for more specific chatroom capabilities.

Thank you fall your feedback and thoughts around this! Keep voting and helping us build our future roadmap.

posts.author: Tuko (Luis Alberto Ruiz)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have spoken with a couple of Monday users, and we all agree on this.

Sometimes, finding a conversation with your team can be a little frustrating. Worst if we are working on a couple of projects at a time.

It will be good to have a general chat, where you can invite some team members and discuss a project. Or a general chat for any type of discussion.

Some of the options I received is to use Slack for communication and Monday for projects.

I think paying twice for a function that is obvious is needed, is not a solution.
Slack, Trello, Twist App; SAAS that are inferior to Monday has that function.

Love Monday, it’s a great SAAS, I hope the team works on this.

And please don’t make it an integration service and make us pay more for each member.

Enhance our team’s communication.

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
hi @Tuko

Doesn’t the board discussion support your needs? It works like the item discussion, except it is at board level.

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
Yes! Make sure to vote for the relevant requests about board discussions so that we can continue to provide status on the features our community wants to see the most!

I will merge this request with Board level discussion

posts.author: PluginGenie (Oliver Albrecht)
posts.cookedHtml:
Good news!

Chat Central was just released to the marketplace: monday.com: Apps Marketplace and I believe would fit your needs well.

It’s a fully featured real-time chat app in monday.com that includes:

  • Custom channels
  • One-on-one messaging
  • Message threads
  • Reactions
  • Gifs
  • @ mentions

In addition, it’s deeply integrated into monday.com. It’s available in:

  • Workspaces: A full-featured chat with custom channels and one-on-one messaging, which also allows for cross-organization collaboration
  • Boards: Chat that can be added to a specific board, for discussions about the specific board
  • Automations: Create a board automation, which automatically adds a message to a channel when an event happens in monday.com (e.g. an item is created/update).

If you have any questions or feedback, feel free to reach out to me directly at: oalbrecht@bonfireapps.com.

posts.author: AlexRevApps (Alexander Infante)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey

Great idea about improving team communication on monday.com! As a monday.com partner, I completely relate to what you’re saying. This need actually inspired me to create an app called SecureChat Pro.

It adds lightweight chat functionality directly within monday.com. It’s designed to enable those quick conversations without leaving the platform. I’m also very focused on security, so everything is fully encrypted end to end.Some features that sound like they could help you are:

  • Privacy settings that allow you to invite specific team members, or leave it open to the entire group
  • Online and typing statuses so you know when people are active
  • Notifications for mentions/ tagging (@name) and notifications when someone is added to a private chat
  • Emojis for reactions and natively
  • Media sharing (PDFs, images, videos, audio files, and GIFs)
  • More features are about to be rolled out this week too!

It’s available in the app marketplace with a 14 day free trial

auth.monday.com

SecureChat Pro - Private Chat - monday.com App Marketplace

Private & Encrypted Messaging for Your Team 🔒 🚀

I’m really big on giving too- so we fit this to be an affordable price. If you do want a further discount, let’s find a time to connect and I’d be happy to chat about that as well.

Feel free to email me at alexander@revapps.io

posts.author: MickaylaM (Mickayla Murray)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello everyone! We’re happy to share that board-level discussions have officially been released.

While you can still use item updates for task-specific conversations, you can now communicate across the entire board using Board Discussions. This new feature makes it easy to share general updates, ideas, and ongoing conversations at the board level — keeping context and collaboration in one place, right where the work happens.

This may not replace real-time chat entirely, but it does address the core need of centralizing broader board conversations inside monday.com. You can check out the full details here: https://support.monday.com/hc/en-us/articles/115005317249-The-basics-of-a-board. We’d love to hear how it’s working for your team!
